summary: Clear KPFK radio capture of a scorching May 1990 show with a killer Queen Jane Approximately and a smoking Help>Slip>Franklin's, plus strong Jerry solos.
review: The recording, taken from the KPFK FM broadcast, is surprisingly clean for an audience‑sourced tape and preserves the heat‑induced laid‑back vibe of the night.  Highlights include an unforgettable version of “Queen Jane Approximately,” a solid “Race Is On” segue into a decent Help>Slip>Franklin's, and standout Jerry Garcia solos on “Loser” and “Touch of Gray.”  While a few jams (especially the first “Help/Slip”) feel a bit sloppy, the overall performance is energetic and memorable.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is 4.56/5 across nine reviews; most praise the musical performance and note good sound quality despite being a radio (audience) source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded from KPFK FM broadcast; reviewers describe the mix as clear and great, though live mix sounded slightly better. No major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Heat‑driven, laid‑back first set that builds into a cooking second set; strong segues like Race Is On into Help/Slip/Franklin's keep the momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solos on Loser and Touch of Gray are praised; some reviewers note occasional sloppiness but overall his playing is a highlight.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as funky and clear throughout, adding solid low‑end to the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ/keyboard solo on Queen Jane Approximately gets special mention; his organ “growls” through the show.
